meta.notes: AI-generated proof-of-concept CPBS items for SpeedyCAT (chunk 1 of 10). NOT real MCAT/AAMC content and NOT from any answer key. Every stem and choice is plain text (no HTML/SVG/LaTeX). Answer keys were computed programmatically and each item passed a 4-criteria eval (four distinct choices; clean plain-text formatting; MCAT-appropriate difficulty; exactly one correct choice).

questions.stem: An object starts at 5 m/s and accelerates uniformly at 3 m/s^2 for 5 s. What is its final velocity?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A current of 5 A flows through a 3 Ω resistor. What is the voltage across it?
questions.explanation: Ohm's law: V = I R = (5)(3) = 15 V.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A wave has frequency 256 Hz and wavelength 2 m. What is its wave speed?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: According to VSEPR theory, what is the molecular geometry of CO2?
questions.explanation: CO2 adopts a linear geometry based on its bonding and lone-pair electron domains.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the approximate molar mass of H2O? (Use H=1, C=12, N=14, O=16, Na=23, Cl=35.5, S=32, K=39, Ca=40, Mg=24, Fe=56, Ag=108)
questions.explanation: Summing the atomic masses in H2O gives a molar mass of about 18 g/mol.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A system absorbs 1000 J of heat and does 200 J of work on its surroundings. What is the change in its internal energy?
questions.explanation: First law: ΔU = Q - W = 1000 - 200 = 800 J.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many molecules are in 0.1 mol of a substance? (Avogadro's number = 6.022 x 10^23 /mol)

questions.stem: The bond in NaCl has an electronegativity difference of about 2.1. It is best classified as
questions.explanation: An electronegativity difference near 2.1 indicates a ionic bond (roughly: 0 nonpolar covalent, up to about 1.7 polar covalent, larger values ionic).
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many moles correspond to 1.2 x 10^24 molecules? (Avogadro's number = 6.022 x 10^23 /mol)
questions.explanation: moles = molecules / N_A = 1.2 x 10^24 / (6.022 x 10^23) = 2 mol.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A reaction proceeds in two steps with ΔH1 = -400 kJ and ΔH2 = -50 kJ. If the overall reaction is the sum of these steps, what is the overall ΔH?
questions.explanation: By Hess's law, ΔH_overall = ΔH1 + ΔH2 = -400 + -50 = -450 kJ.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the strongest type of intermolecular force present in pure H2O?

questions.stem: What is the mass percent of H in H2O?
questions.explanation: mass percent = (mass of element / molar mass) x 100 = (2/18) x 100 = 11.1%.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A gas at 3 atm occupies 1 L. At constant temperature, what volume does it occupy at 0.5 atm?
questions.explanation: Boyle's law: P1 V1 = P2 V2, so V2 = (3)(1)/0.5 = 6 L.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the equilibrium constant expression K for the reaction N2 + 3 H2 ⇌ 2 NH3?
questions.explanation: K places products over reactants, each concentration raised to its coefficient: K = [NH3]^2 / ([N2][H2]^3).
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An ionic bond forms primarily by

choices.label: A
choices.text: transfer of electrons from a metal to a nonmetal.

choices.label: B
choices.text: overlap of half-filled p orbitals in a noble gas.

choices.label: C
choices.text: a sea of delocalized electrons among metal atoms.

choices.label: D
choices.text: equal sharing of electrons between two nonmetals.

questions.explanation: Ionic bonding involves electron transfer producing cations and anions held by electrostatic attraction.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A sample has mass 200 g and volume 15 mL. What is its density?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A gas occupies 10 L at 400 K. At constant pressure, what volume does it occupy at 300 K?
questions.explanation: Charles's law: V1/T1 = V2/T2, so V2 = 10 x 300/400 = 7.5 L.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: According to Le Chatelier's principle, adding more reactant to a system at equilibrium generally

choices.label: A
choices.text: shifts the equilibrium toward the reactants.

choices.label: B
choices.text: shifts the equilibrium toward the products.

choices.label: C
choices.text: causes no shift in the position of equilibrium.

choices.label: D
choices.text: shifts toward the side with more moles of gas.

questions.explanation: The system opposes the change: adding more reactant to a system at equilibrium shifts the equilibrium toward the products.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An exothermic reaction is one that

choices.label: A
choices.text: releases heat to the surroundings (negative ΔH).

choices.label: B
choices.text: always lowers the entropy of the universe.

choices.label: C
choices.text: does not involve any energy change.

choices.label: D
choices.text: absorbs heat from the surroundings (positive ΔH).

questions.explanation: Exothermic reactions release energy, giving a negative enthalpy change.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An ideal fluid flows at 2 m/s through a pipe of cross-section 4 cm^2. What is its speed where the cross-section narrows to 1 cm^2?

questions.stem: Which atom has the larger atomic radius, Na or Cl?

choices.text: atomic radius shows no periodic trend
